ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning the waters.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Water also is one of the four elements, the most beautiful of God’s creations.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is both wet and cold, heavy, and with a tendency to descend, and flows with great readiness.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is this the Holy Scripture has in view when it says, And darkness was upon the face of the deep.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And the Spirit of God moved upon the face of the waters.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the deep is nothing else than a huge quantity of water whose limit man cannot comprehend.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In the beginning, indeed, the water lay all over the surface of the earth.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And first God created the firmament to divide the water above the firmament from the water below the firmament.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For in the midst of the sea of waters the firmament was established at the Master’s decree.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And out of it God bade the firmament arise, and it arose.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now for what reason was it that God placed water above the firmament?

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It was because of the intense burning heat of the sun and ether.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For immediately under the firmament is spread out the ether, and the sun and moon and stars are in the firmament, and so if water had not been put above it the firmament would have been consumed by the heat.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Next, God bade the waters be gathered together into one mass.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But when the Scripture speaks of one mass it evidently does not mean that they were gathered together into one place: for immediately it goes on to say, And the gatherings of the waters He called seas: but the words signify that the waters were separated off in a body from the earth into distinct groups.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Thus the waters were gathered together into their special collections and the dry land was brought to view.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And hence arose the two seas that surround Egypt, for it lies between two seas.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: These collections contain various seas and mountains, and islands, and promontories, and harbours, and surround various bays and beaches, and coastlands.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the word beach is used when the nature of the tract is sandy, while coastland signifies that it is rocky and deep close into shore, getting deep all on a sudden.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In like manner arose also the sea that lies where the sun rises, the name of which is the Indian Sea: also the northern sea called the Caspian.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The lakes also were formed in the same manner.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The ocean, then, is like a river encircling the whole earth, and I think it is concerning it that the divine Scripture says, A river went out of Paradise.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The water of the ocean is sweet and potable.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is it that furnishes the seas with water which, because it stays a long time in the seas and stands unmoved, becomes bitter: for the sun and the waterspouts draw up always the finer parts.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Thus it is that clouds are formed and showers take place, because the filtration makes the water sweet.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: This is parted into four first divisions, that is to say, into four rivers.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The name of the first is Pheison, which is the Indian Ganges; the name of the second is Geon, which is the Nile flowing from Ethiopia down to Egypt: the name of the third is Tigris, and the name of the fourth is Euphrates.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: There are also very many other mighty rivers of which some empty themselves into the sea and others are used up in the earth.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Thus the whole earth is bored through and mined, and has, so to speak, certain veins through which it sends up in springs the water it has received from the sea.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The water of the spring thus depends for its character on the quality of the earth.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the sea water is filtered and strained through the earth and thus becomes sweet.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But if the place from which the spring arises is bitter or briny, so also is the water that is sent up.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Moreover, it often happens that water which has been closely pent up bursts through with violence, and thus it becomes warm.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And this is why they send forth waters that are naturally warm.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: By the divine decree hollow places are made in the earth, and so into these the waters are gathered.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And this is how mountains are formed.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: God, then, bade the first water produce living breath, since it was to be by water and the Holy Spirit that moved upon the waters in the beginning, that man was to be renewed.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For this is what the divine Basilius said: Therefore it produced living creatures, small and big; whales and dragons, fish that swim in the waters, and feathered fowl.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The birds form a link between water and earth and air: for they have their origin in the water, they live on the earth and they fly in the air.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Water, then, is the most beautiful element and rich in usefulness, and purifies from all filth, and not only from the filth of the body but from that of the soul, if it should have received the grace of the Spirit.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Concerning the seas.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Ægean Sea is received by the Hellespont, which ends at Abydos and Sestus: next, the Propontis, which ends at Chalcedon and Byzantium: here are the straits where the Pontus arises.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Next, the lake of Mæotis.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Again, from the beginning of Europe and Libya it is the Iberian Sea, which extends from the pillars of Hercules to the Pyrenees mountain.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Then the Ligurian Sea as far as the borders of Etruria.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Next, the Sardinian Sea, which is above Sardinia and inclines downwards to Libya.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Then the Etrurian Sea, which begins at the extreme limits of Liguria and ends at Sicily.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Then the Libyan Sea.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Then the Cretan, and Sicilian, and Ionian, and Adriatic Seas, the last of which is poured out of the Sicilian Sea, which is called the Corinthian Gulf, or the Alcyonian Sea.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Saronic Sea is surrounded by the Sunian and Scyllæan Seas.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Next is the Myrtoan Sea and the Icarian Sea, in which are also the Cyclades.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Then the Carpathian, and Pamphylian, and Egyptian Seas: and, thereafter, above the Icarian Sea, the Ægean Sea pours itself out.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: There is also the coast of Europe from the mouth of the Tanais River to the Pillars of Hercules, 609,709 stadia: and that of Libya from the Tigris, as far as the mouth of the Canobus, 209,252 stadia: and lastly, that of Asia from the Canobus to the Tanais, which, including the Gulf, is 4,111 stadia.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And so the full extent of the seaboard of the world that we inhabit with the gulfs is 1,309,072 stadia.
